ogTitle: Doyalson vs Doyalson North: Climate Safety

heading: Doyalson vs Doyalson North — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Doyalson, New South Wales and Doyalson North, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Doyalson has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Doyalson North (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Doyalson or Doyalson North safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Doyalson has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Doyalson North scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Doyalson scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Doyalson North scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Doyalson or Doyalson North?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Doyalson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
